title: astrantia major subsp. biebersteinii
intro: Astrantia major subsp. biebersteinii, also known as Great Masterwort, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the temperate regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The herbal preparations of Astrantia major subsp. biebersteinii can be found in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
